# 7-Day Meal Plan
### For a 31-Year-Old Australian Adult
**Allergies/Intolerances:** Shellfish, Gluten, Lactose, Onion
**Preferences:** Meat, Potatoes, Mexican, Thai, Indian flavours
**Sensitivity:** Super-taster — mild, adjustable seasoning throughout
**Budget:** Cheap and simple

---

> **A few quick notes before we start:**
> - "Adjustable" means seasoning is added at the table or kept minimal in cooking — you control the heat and intensity
> - All dairy used is **lactose-free** (lactose-free milk, lactose-free yoghurt, dairy-free butter/coconut oil)
> - All grains/starches used are **naturally gluten-free** (rice, corn tortillas, potatoes, oats labelled GF)
> - Onion is completely omitted — garlic and the **green tops of spring onions only** are used where flavour depth is needed (the green tops are low-FODMAP and generally well tolerated by onion-intolerant people — skip even these if you react)
> - Portions are designed for one adult
> - Costs are approximate AUD estimates based on typical Coles/Woolworths pricing

### **Important Notes Before You Start**

*   **Gluten-Free:** Always check labels on sauces, spices, and pre-packaged goods for hidden gluten. Use certified gluten-free tamari instead of soy sauce.
*   **Lactose-Free:** This plan uses no dairy. For creaminess, it relies on coconut milk, avocado, and oil-based dressings.
*   **Onion Substitute:** Asafoetida powder (hing) is a classic Indian substitute for onion/garlic flavour. Use it sparingly (a pinch is enough). Find it in the spice aisle of Indian grocery stores or major supermarkets. Alternatively, use the green part of spring onions (scallions) for a fresh, mild flavour, as the intolerance is usually to the bulb.
*   **Super-Taster & Seasoning:** All seasonings are added at the end or are "adjustable." Start with half the amount of spice listed, taste, and add more if you can tolerate it. This puts you in control.
*   **Salt & Sugar:** The plan avoids adding salty sauces and sugary dressings. Flavour comes from herbs, spices, acids (lemon/lime), and healthy fats.

---

### **7-Day Meal Plan**

**Day 1**
*   **Breakfast:** Scrambled eggs with spinach and a slice of gluten-free toast.
*   **Lunch:** **Mexican Beef & Potato Bowls.** Browned mince and diced potatoes cooked with cumin, paprika, and a pinch of chilli powder (optional). Served with rice, diced avocado, and fresh coriander.
*   **Dinner:** Leftover Mexican beef and potato bowls.
*   **Snack:** Apple slices with almond butter.

**Day 2**
*   **Breakfast:** Gluten-free porridge with sliced banana and a drizzle of maple syrup.
*   **Lunch:** **Deconstructed Taco Salad.** Leftover Mexican beef mix on a bed of lettuce with cherry tomatoes, cucumber, avocado, and a squeeze of lime juice.
*   **Dinner:** **Simple Thai Green Curry.** Chicken and vegetables simmered in coconut milk and Thai green curry paste. Serve with jasmine rice.
*   **Snack:** Rice cakes with avocado slices.

**Day 3**
*   **Breakfast:** Chia seed pudding with coconut milk and berries.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Thai Green Curry.
*   **Dinner:** **Shepherd's Pie.** Beef mince and mixed vegetables in a gluten-free gravy, topped with mashed potatoes.
*   **Snack:** Handful of mixed nuts.

**Day 4**
*   **Breakfast:** Smoothie with oat milk, banana, spinach, and protein powder.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Shepherd's Pie.
*   **Dinner:** **Lemon Herb Roast Chicken & Veg.** Whole chicken or chicken pieces roasted with lemon, rosemary, and carrots/potatoes.
*   **Snack:** Pear slices with pumpkin seeds.

**Day 5**
*   **Breakfast:** Two fried eggs with sliced tomato on gluten-free toast.
*   **Lunch:** Shredded roast chicken mixed with dairy-free mayo and celery in lettuce cups.
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Yellow Dahl.** Red lentils cooked with turmeric, cumin, asafoetida, and ginger. Serve with rice and steamed green beans.
*   **Snack:** Carrot and cucumber sticks with hummus.

**Day 6**
*   **Breakfast:** Gluten-free porridge with berries.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Mild Yellow Dahl.
*   **Dinner:** **"Build-Your-Own" Baked Potato Bar.** Baked potatoes topped with shredded chicken, black beans, corn, avocado, and lactose-free sour cream.
*   **Snack:** Mixed frozen berries.

**Day 7**
*   **Breakfast:** Smoothie or scrambled eggs.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover baked potato with chosen toppings.
*   **Dinner:** **Stir-fried Pork Mince & Green Beans.** Pork mince stir-fried with green beans, ginger, and gluten-free tamari. Serve with rice.
*   **Snack:** Plain popcorn popped in oil.

*   Lemons
*   Limes

scaffold_text:
Below is a cheap, simple 7‑day meal plan that avoids shellfish, gluten, lactose and onion. It uses meat, potatoes and Mexican / Thai / Indian–style flavors, keeps seasoning mild and adjustable for super‑taster sensitivity, and avoids very salty or very sweet foods. Where a normally common ingredient could contain gluten, lactose or onion (for example regular soy sauce, stock cubes or spice mixes), I’ve noted label checks or alternatives. Each day lists breakfast, lunch, dinner and one or two simple snacks.

Quick notes before the plan
- Always check labels: look for “gluten‑free” on oats, tortillas, soy sauce/tamari, miso and stock; check “lactose‑free” if buying dairy replacements. Avoid anything listing wheat, barley, rye or malt.
- No onion or onion powder anywhere. I also avoid shallots/leeks/green onion to be safe.
- Keep seasoning mild: start with very small amounts of salt and spice, taste, and add more at the table if needed. Offer chili flakes, extra tamari or lime at the table for extra heat/umami.
- Use gluten‑free tamari or coconut aminos instead of regular soy sauce. Use canned coconut milk for Thai/Indian dishes.
- If you want garlic flavor but don’t tolerate alliums, you can omit garlic or use a small amount of garlic‑infused oil (not suitable if you’re sensitive to all alliums). I leave garlic optional for each recipe. Asafoetida (hing) is a traditional onion/garlic substitute for some Indian dishes — only use if you can obtain a labeled gluten‑free product.

Day 1 (Mexican, simple)
- Breakfast: Overnight oats (gluten‑free oats + plant milk, 1 tsp chia, sliced banana, cinnamon); add small handful of berries if available.
- Lunch: Roast chicken thigh (leftover) + potato salad (roasted potato cubes, chopped cucumber, chopped tomato, chopped cilantro, lime & 1 tsp olive oil, pepper). Light salt—taste first.
- Dinner: Mild beef mince tacos: brown minced beef with ground cumin, smoked paprika, mild chili powder (very small pinch), salt to taste; serve on warmed gluten‑free corn tortillas with tomato‑cilantro salsa (tomato + cilantro + lime + pepper + pinch salt if wanted) and mashed avocado.
- Snacks: Apple; handful of roasted peanuts or mixed nuts.

Day 2 (Thai light)
- Breakfast: Scrambled eggs with baby spinach and tomato; small sprinkle of black pepper.
- Lunch: Leftover tacos as a bowl: warmed rice + leftover minced beef + avocado + tomato.
- Dinner: Mild coconut chicken curry: diced chicken thighs, 1 can coconut milk, diced potato, sliced carrots, grated ginger (~1 tsp), turmeric (¼–½ tsp), ground coriander & cumin (each ¼ tsp), salt to taste; simmer until potatoes tender. Serve with rice. Add lime at table.
- Snacks: Banana; rice crackers (gluten‑free).

Day 3 (Indian style)
- Breakfast: Potato & spinach hash: diced pan‑fried potato with turmeric (pinch), cumin (small pinch), pepper; top with a fried egg.
- Lunch: Chickpea salad: canned chickpeas (rinsed), chopped cucumber, tomato, coriander, lemon juice, a drizzle of olive oil, ground cumin (very small pinch). Mild seasoning.
- Dinner: Keema‑style mince: minced beef or lamb, tomato, turmeric, ground cumin & coriander, garam masala (low amount), fresh ginger; simmer and serve over rice or with roasted potatoes. Optional: a pinch of hing (asafoetida) if you use it.
- Snacks: Plain plant‑yogurt (unsweetened) with a few sliced banana or berries.

Day 4 (Mexican / roast)
- Breakfast: Toasted gluten‑free corn tortilla with mashed avocado, squeeze lime, and a soft‑boiled or fried egg.
- Lunch: Leftover keema over rice or as a salad with lettuce and tomato.
- Dinner: Lime‑paprika baked chicken drumsticks, roast potatoes and steamed green beans (season lightly with pepper and small pinch of salt).
- Snacks: Carrot sticks with a small pot of garlic‑free hummus (or mashed avocado).

Day 5 (Thai / peanut)
- Breakfast: Smoothie: plant milk + banana + a small spoon peanut butter + spinach (optional) — keep tastes mild.
- Lunch: Rice noodle salad (use rice noodles): shredded poached chicken, grated carrot, cucumber ribbons, chopped cilantro, lime juice and 1 tsp gluten‑free tamari or coconut aminos, a light drizzle of oil. Keep dressing minimal; add more at table if wanted.
- Dinner: Mild peanut satay: make sauce from peanut butter + coconut milk + lime + small tamari splash + grated ginger; stir into cooked chicken pieces and steamed broccoli; serve with rice. Start with 1 tbsp peanut butter per 1 cup coconut milk and adjust, keep it mild.
- Snacks: Boiled egg; small handful of almonds.

Day 6 (Indian / hearty)
- Breakfast: Chickpea “scramble”: mashed chickpeas, turmeric, cumin and tomato, warmed; serve on roasted potato slices or gluten‑free toast.
- Lunch: Leftover peanut chicken over rice or with salad.
- Dinner: Aloo (potato) & beef curry: chunky potatoes, minced beef or diced beef, canned tomatoes, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala (low), water to simmer; serve with rice. Taste gently and add salt sparingly.
- Snacks: Popcorn (air‑popped, light salt) or an orange.

Day 7 (simple & flexible)
- Breakfast: Warm porridge (gluten‑free oats) with plant milk, cinnamon, and sliced banana.
- Lunch: Leftover curry or chicken in a gluten‑free wrap with lettuce, grated carrot and a smear of mashed avocado.
- Dinner: Grilled steak or beef patties (lightly seasoned with pepper, herbs), simple mashed potato (with plant milk and olive oil instead of butter), and steamed greens (broccoli or spinach) with lemon.
- Snacks: Sliced apple with peanut butter; plain plant‑yogurt if desired.

Simple portioning/leftover strategy
- Cook rice in bulk (3–4 cups dry) early in the week, refrigerate in portions.
- Roast a tray of potatoes and some chicken at start of week for quick builds.
- Use one big pot of

- Dinner: Mild peanut satay: make sauce from peanut butter + coconut milk + lime + small tamari splash + grated ginger; stir into cooked chicken pieces and steamed broccoli; serve with rice. Start with 1 tbsp peanut butter per 1 cup coconut milk and adjust, keep it mild.
- Snacks: Boiled egg; small handful of almonds.

Day 6 (Indian / hearty)
- Breakfast: Chickpea “scramble”: mashed chickpeas, turmeric, cumin and tomato, warmed; serve on roasted potato slices or gluten‑free toast.
- Lunch: Leftover peanut chicken over rice or with salad.
- Dinner: Aloo (potato) & beef curry: chunky potatoes, minced beef or diced beef, canned tomatoes, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala (low), water to simmer; serve with rice. Taste gently and add salt sparingly.
- Snacks: Popcorn (air‑popped, light salt) or an orange.

Day 7 (simple & flexible)
- Breakfast: Warm porridge (gluten‑free oats) with plant milk, cinnamon, and sliced banana.
- Lunch: Leftover curry or chicken in a gluten‑free wrap with lettuce, grated carrot and a smear of mashed avocado.
- Dinner: Grilled steak or beef patties (lightly seasoned with pepper, herbs), simple mashed potato (with plant milk and olive oil instead of butter), and steamed greens (broccoli or spinach) with lemon.
- Snacks: Sliced apple with peanut butter; plain plant‑yogurt if desired.

- Cook rice in bulk (3–4 cups dry) early in the week, refrigerate in portions.

Day 1  
 Breakfast  
  • Overnight oats: gluten-free oats, almond milk, cinnamon, sliced banana  
 Lunch  
  • Chicken salad: shredded roast chicken, mixed greens, cucumber, tomato, olive-oil & lemon dressing  
 Dinner  
  • Thai-style coconut chicken soup: chicken pieces, sliced carrot, capsicum, garlic, ginger, light coconut milk, rice noodles (season with fish sauce*, lime, mild chilli to taste)  
 Snack  
  • Apple slices + handful almonds  
 Prep note  
  • Soak oats overnight. Roast/braise extra chicken for salads and Day 3. Cook extra rice noodles.

Day 2  
 Breakfast  
  • Green smoothie: spinach, frozen berries, banana, almond milk, a pinch of cinnamon  
 Lunch  
  • Beef & black-bean rice bowl: ground beef (mildly seasoned with cumin, paprika), canned black beans (rinsed), chopped tomato, avocado, squeeze of lime  
 Dinner  
  • Indian-style chicken & potato curry: chicken pieces, cubed potato, peas, garlic, ginger, turmeric, mild curry powder, light coconut milk; serve with steamed rice  
 Snack  
  • Carrot sticks + homemade hummus (chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon, olive oil, salt)  
 Prep note  
  • Cook extra rice for Day 4. Make hummus in advance.

Day 3  
 Breakfast  
  • Scrambled eggs with baby spinach and cherry-tomato halves  
 Lunch  
  • GF chicken tacos: warmed corn tortillas, shredded leftover roast chicken, lettuce, tomato, avocado, lime; optional mild salsa  
 Dinner  
  • Potato & capsicum hash: diced potato & capsicum pan-fried in oil, seasoned with paprika, garlic, salt & pepper; serve with a fried egg (or two)  
 Snack  
  • GF rice cakes + peanut butter  
 Prep note  
  • Dice potatoes/capsicum and store in fridge.

Day 4  
 Breakfast  
  • Chia pudding: chia seeds soaked overnight in coconut milk, topped with fresh berries or banana slices  
 Lunch  
  • Warm potato & egg salad: boiled potatoes, chopped hard-boiled eggs, chopped parsley/cilantro, olive oil, mustard, lemon  
 Dinner  
  • Mexican lentil chili: red lentils, canned tomatoes, diced capsicum, garlic, cumin, coriander, mild chilli powder; serve with corn tortilla chips  
 Snack  
  • Banana + handful walnuts  
 Prep note  
  • Pre-cook red lentils.

Day 5  
 Breakfast  
  • GF toast with mashed avocado, tomato slices, pinch of salt & pepper  
 Lunch  
  • Thai peanut chicken lettuce wraps: minced chicken stir-fried with grated carrot & garlic, tossed with a mild peanut sauce (peanut butter, light soy-free tamari, lime, water)  
 Dinner  
  • Simple dhal: red lentils cooked with turmeric, garlic, ginger; finish with a splash of coconut milk; serve with rice or leftover GF bread  
 Snack  
  • Cucumber sticks + guacamole (avocado, tomato, lime, garlic)  
 Prep note  
  • Stir-fry extra minced chicken for Day 7.

Day 6  
 Breakfast  
  • Mango-coconut smoothie bowl: frozen mango, coconut milk, topped with chia or sliced banana  
 Lunch  
  • Leftover dhal or chicken curry, reheated, served with rice or lettuce wraps  
 Dinner  
  • Beef & potato stir-fry: sliced beef, diced potato (pre-boiled), capsicum, garlic, light soy-free tamari, mild chilli to taste; serve over rice  
 Snack  
  • Mixed nuts & dried fruit  
 Prep note  
  • Pre-boil potatoes for faster stir-fry.

Day 7  
 Breakfast  
  • Omelette: eggs, diced capsicum & tomato, chopped cilantro  
 Lunch  
  • Chicken & potato patties: leftover shredded chicken + mashed potato + egg + gluten-free flour (or ground oats) → pan-fry; serve with side salad  
 Dinner  
  • Mexican stuffed peppers: halved capsicum filled with rice, black beans, tomato, cumin, coriander; bake until tender  
 Snack  
  • Fresh fruit salad (banana, apple, berries)  
 Prep note  
  • Mash leftover potatoes; core peppers in advance.

Grocery List

Produce  
 • Bananas (7)  
 • Apples (3-4)  
 • Mixed berries (fresh or frozen)  
 • Mango (frozen or fresh)  
 • Spinach (1 bag)  
 • Mixed salad greens or lettuce (1 head)  
 • Cucumber (2)  
 • Tomatoes (6-8)  
 • Cherry tomatoes (1 punnet)  
 • Capsicum/bell peppers (6)  
 • Carrots (1 bag)  
 • Potatoes (1.5 kg)  
 • Avocados (4)  
 • Limes (3)  
 • Garlic (1 bulb)  
 • Fresh ginger (small piece)  
 • Fresh cilantro/coriander (1 bunch)  

Proteins & Dairy Alternatives  
 • Chicken thighs or breasts (1.2 kg)  
 • Minced beef (or turkey) (500 g)  
 • Eggs (12)  
 • Canned black beans (2 cans)  
 • Canned chickpeas (1 can)  
 • Red lentils (500 g)  
 • Almond milk (1 L)  
 • Light coconut milk (2 cans)  
 • Peanut butter (no added sugar/salt)  

Grains & Snacks  
 • Gluten-free oats  
 • Gluten-free bread or wraps (1 loaf/pack)  
 • Corn tortillas (1 pack)  
 • Rice (1 kg)  
 • Rice noodles (200 g)  
 • GF rice cakes  
 • Chia seeds  
 • Mixed nuts (almonds, walnuts, etc.)  
 • Dried fruit or raisins  
 • Corn tortilla chips (optional)  

Oils & Condiments  
 • Olive oil  
 • Canola or vegetable oil  
 • Tahini  
 • Soy-free tamari (gluten-free) or GF soy sauce  
 • Mustard (check for gluten)  

Spices & Seasonings  
 • Salt (use sparingly)  
 • Black pepper  
 • Cinnamon  
 • Cumin powder  
 • Coriander powder  
 • Turmeric  
 • Paprika  
 • Mild chilli powder (optional)  
 • Curry powder (mild)  
 • Garam masala  
 • Dried oregano  

Basic Weekly Prep Notes  
 1. Cook a large batch of rice; store in fridge up to 4 days.  
 2. Pre-soak chia and overnight oats.  
 3. Roast/braise half the chicken; reserve for salads and tacos.  
 4. Pre-boil potatoes for quicker hashes, patties and stir-fries.  
 5. Pre-cook red lentils for dhal and chili.  
 6. Chop all veggies (capsicum, carrots, cucumbers, potatoes) on Day 1; keep in sealed containers.  
 7. Make hummus and guacamole early in the week; stir before serving.

Seasoning tip: always start with small amounts of salt, chilli or spice blends; let each eater adjust to taste.  
Enjoy simple, flavourful meals that fit your dietary needs and budget!

 • Soy-free tamari  
 • Mustard  

Spices & Seasonings  
 • Salt  
 • Black pepper  
 • Cinnamon  
 • Turmeric  
 • Paprika  
 • Mild chili powder  
 • Cumin  
 • Coconut milk  
 • Garam masala  

Basic Weekly Prep Notes  
 1. Pre-cook rice and potatoes for faster meal prep.  
 2. Pre-cook red lentils for curry.  
 3. Core peppers in advance for easier meal prep.  
 4. Soak oats overnight for easy breakfast.  
 5. Roast extra chicken for salads.  
 6. Cook extra beef for Day 7.  
Enjoy a week of delicious, allergy-friendly meals with your favorite flavors!
scaffold_text:
7-Day Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Onion-Free Meal Plan  
(Uses mild, adjustable seasoning; avoid very salty/sweet; budget-friendly)

Day 1  
 Breakfast  
  • Overnight oats: gluten-free oats, almond milk, cinnamon, sliced banana  
 Lunch  
  • Chicken salad: shredded roast chicken, mixed greens, cucumber, tomato, olive-oil & lemon dressing  
 Dinner  
  • Thai-style coconut chicken soup: chicken pieces, sliced carrot, capsicum, garlic, ginger, light coconut milk, rice noodles (season with fish sauce*, lime, mild chilli to taste)  
 Snack  
  • Apple slices + handful almonds  
 Prep note  
  • Soak oats overnight. Roast/braise extra chicken for salads and Day 3. Cook extra rice noodles.

Day 2  
 Breakfast  
  • Green smoothie: spinach, frozen berries, banana, almond milk, a pinch of cinnamon  
 Lunch  
  • Beef & black-bean rice bowl: ground beef (mildly seasoned with cumin, paprika), canned black beans (rinsed), chopped tomato, avocado, squeeze of lime  
 Dinner  
  • Indian-style chicken & potato curry: chicken pieces, cubed potato, peas, garlic, ginger, turmeric, mild curry powder, light coconut milk; serve with steamed rice  
 Snack  
  • Carrot sticks + homemade hummus (chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon, olive oil, salt)  
 Prep note  
  • Cook extra rice for Day 4. Make hummus in advance.

Day 3  
 Breakfast  
  • Scrambled eggs with baby spinach and cherry-tomato halves  
 Lunch  
  • GF chicken tacos: warmed corn tortillas, shredded leftover roast chicken, lettuce, tomato, avocado, lime; optional mild salsa  
 Dinner  
  • Potato & capsicum hash: diced potato & capsicum pan-fried in oil, seasoned with paprika, garlic, salt & pepper; serve with a fried egg (or two)  
 Snack  
  • GF rice cakes + peanut butter  
 Prep note  
  • Dice potatoes/capsicum and store in fridge.

Day 4  
 Breakfast  
  • Chia pudding: chia seeds soaked overnight in coconut milk, topped with fresh berries or banana slices  
 Lunch  
  • Warm potato & egg salad: boiled potatoes, chopped hard-boiled eggs, chopped parsley/cilantro, olive oil, mustard, lemon  
 Dinner  
  • Mexican lentil chili: red lentils, canned tomatoes, diced capsicum, garlic, cumin, coriander, mild chilli powder; serve with corn tortilla chips  
 Snack  
  • Banana + handful walnuts  
 Prep note  
  • Pre-cook red lentils.

Day 5  
 Breakfast  
  • GF toast with mashed avocado, tomato slices, pinch of salt & pepper  
 Lunch  
  • Thai peanut chicken lettuce wraps: minced chicken stir-fried with grated carrot & garlic, tossed with a mild peanut sauce (peanut butter, light soy-free tamari, lime, water)  
 Dinner  
  • Simple dhal: red lentils cooked with turmeric, garlic, ginger; finish with a splash of coconut milk; serve with rice or leftover GF bread  
 Snack  
  • Cucumber sticks + guacamole (avocado, tomato, lime, garlic)  
 Prep note  
  • Stir-fry extra minced chicken for Day 7.

Day 6  
 Breakfast  
  • Mango-coconut smoothie bowl: frozen mango, coconut milk, topped with chia or sliced banana  
 Lunch  
  • Leftover dhal or chicken curry, reheated, served with rice or lettuce wraps  
 Dinner  
  • Beef & potato stir-fry: sliced beef, diced potato (pre-boiled), capsicum, garlic, light soy-free tamari, mild chilli to taste; serve over rice  
 Snack  
  • Mixed nuts & dried fruit  
 Prep note  
  • Pre-boil potatoes for faster stir-fry.

Day 7  
 Breakfast  
  • Omelette: eggs, diced capsicum & tomato, chopped cilantro  
 Lunch  
  • Chicken & potato patties: leftover shredded chicken + mashed potato + egg + gluten-free flour (or ground oats) → pan-fry; serve with side salad  
 Dinner  
  • Mexican stuffed peppers: halved capsicum filled with rice, black beans, tomato, cumin, coriander; bake until tender  
 Snack  
  • Fresh fruit salad (banana, apple, berries)  
 Prep note  
  • Mash leftover potatoes; core peppers in advance.

Grocery List

Produce  
 • Bananas (7)  
 • Apples (3-4)  
 • Mixed berries (fresh or frozen)  
 • Mango (frozen or fresh)  
 • Spinach (1 bag)  
 • Mixed salad greens or lettuce (1 head)  
 • Cucumber (2)  
 • Tomatoes (6-8)  
 • Cherry tomatoes (1 punnet)  
 • Capsicum/bell peppers (6)  
 • Carrots (1 bag)  
 • Potatoes (1.5 kg)  
 • Avocados (4)  
 • Limes (3)  
 • Garlic (1 bulb)  
 • Fresh ginger (small piece)  
 • Fresh cilantro/coriander (1 bunch)  

Proteins & Dairy Alternatives  
 • Chicken thighs or breasts (1.2 kg)  
 • Minced beef (or turkey) (500 g)  
 • Eggs (12)  
 • Canned black beans (2 cans)  
 • Canned chickpeas (1 can)  
 • Red lentils (500 g)  
 • Almond milk (1 L)  
 • Light coconut milk (2 cans)  
 • Peanut butter (no added sugar/salt)  

Grains & Snacks  
 • Gluten-free oats  
 • Gluten-free bread or wraps (1 loaf/pack)  
 • Corn tortillas (1 pack)  
 • Rice (1 kg)  
 • Rice noodles (200 g)  
 • GF rice cakes  
 • Chia seeds  
 • Mixed nuts (almonds, walnuts, etc.)  
 • Dried fruit or raisins  
 • Corn tortilla chips (optional)  

Oils & Condiments  
 • Olive oil  
 • Canola or vegetable oil  
 • Tahini  
 • Soy-free tamari (gluten-free) or GF soy sauce  
 • Mustard (check for gluten)  

Spices & Seasonings  
 • Salt (use sparingly)  
 • Black pepper  
 • Cinnamon  
 • Cumin powder  
 • Coriander powder  
 • Turmeric  
 • Paprika  
 • Mild chilli powder (optional)  
 • Curry powder (mild)  
 • Garam masala  
 • Dried oregano  

Basic Weekly Prep Notes  
 1. Cook a large batch of rice; store in fridge up to 4 days.  
 2. Pre-soak chia and overnight oats.  
 3. Roast/braise half the chicken; reserve for salads and tacos.  
 4. Pre-boil potatoes for quicker hashes, patties and stir-fries.  
 5. Pre-cook red lentils for dhal and chili.  
 6. Chop all veggies (capsicum, carrots, cucumbers, potatoes) on Day 1; keep in sealed containers.  
 7. Make hummus and guacamole early in the week; stir before serving.

Seasoning tip: always start with small amounts of salt, chilli or spice blends; let each eater adjust to taste.  
Enjoy simple, flavourful meals that fit your dietary needs and budget!
